entries.concept: Retro mountain soundscape with a microphone sun and boxed pop icon
entries.scene: A hand-pulled screenprinted gig poster shows one dominant central motif: a giant circular vintage microphone transformed into a striped eclipse-like sun rising from a mountain meadow. Its ring frame becomes the main halo, and inside the disc the mesh is simplified into bold horizontal bands, with black bird silhouettes cutting across it. Nestled at the base of the microphone-sun is a small open wooden box, cracked wide like a stage hatch, with a stylized adult figure unfolding from it in a fluid crouch; the figure’s face is rendered in punchy pop-art planes with dark hair, black sunglasses, and magenta lips. Orbiting this center are chunky screenprinted graphics: a deep blue biomorphic shape and a hot pink twin shape sweeping like mirrored comet shards through the sky; a continuous-line embracing couple reinterpreted as a glowing contour cut into the clouds and meadow stream; and scattered oversized keyboard-key blocks embedded like stepping stones across the foreground grid-meets-landscape plain. The whole world merges alpine valley, retro grid horizon, and poster-flat studio space into one connected environment, using thick inks, crisp knockouts, and halftone shadows so every motif reads instantly as one bold concert poster image.
entries.prompt: Create one heavy-ink screenprinted gig poster - thick flat colour, hard confident shapes, halftone dots for shading, and absolutely no lettering. The image is a 16:9 music blog header. Scene: A hand-pulled screenprinted gig poster shows one dominant central motif: a giant circular vintage microphone transformed into a striped eclipse-like sun rising from a mountain meadow. Its ring frame becomes the main halo, and inside the disc the mesh is simplified into bold horizontal bands, with black bird silhouettes cutting across it. Nestled at the base of the microphone-sun is a small open wooden box, cracked wide like a stage hatch, with a stylized adult figure unfolding from it in a fluid crouch; the figure’s face is rendered in punchy pop-art planes with dark hair, black sunglasses, and magenta lips. Orbiting this center are chunky screenprinted graphics: a deep blue biomorphic shape and a hot pink twin shape sweeping like mirrored comet shards through the sky; a continuous-line embracing couple reinterpreted as a glowing contour cut into the clouds and meadow stream; and scattered oversized keyboard-key blocks embedded like stepping stones across the foreground grid-meets-landscape plain. The whole world merges alpine valley, retro grid horizon, and poster-flat studio space into one connected environment, using thick inks, crisp knockouts, and halftone shadows so every motif reads instantly as one bold concert poster image. Weave in recognisable elements taken from the 8 uploaded album covers: source 1: Pop-art adult face with black sunglasses and magenta lips; source 2: Blue glossy biomorphic emblem; source 3: Hot pink glossy biomorphic emblem; source 4: Continuous-line embracing adult couple; source 5: Striped green oval with flying bird silhouettes over a grid; source 6: Large circular vintage microphone; source 7: Adult figure emerging from a small wooden box; source 8: Mountain meadow with winding stream and oversized keyboard keys. Transform the subjects, figures, creatures, objects, and symbols depicted in the uploaded covers so they stay recognisable, and place them all inside one cohesive world with a single shared setting and consistent light. Turn each album motif into a bold screenprinted graphic element - chunky shapes, halftone tone, crisp knockouts - each one readable at a glance. Composition: One dominant central motif with the others orbiting it, tight and punchy like a classic gig poster. Colour direction: olive green, hot pink, deep violet-blue, black - three or four flat saturated ink colours on paper. Mood: dreamy, bold, retro-futurist, intimate. Keep the scene safe and tasteful: feature only adults (no children, infants, or minors), and show no gore, wounds, body horror, medical or anatomical imagery, blank or milky eyes, nudity, or sexual content. Reinterpret any such source motif abstractly, and suggest rather than exactly replicate the likeness of any identifiable real person. Do not include any text, letters, words, numbers, captions, titles, logos, watermarks, or signage anywhere in the image. Avoid: readable text, letters, words, numbers, captions, titles, logos, watermarks, signage, grid layout, contact sheet, evenly tiled squares, raw album-cover thumbnails, collage of separate panels, photorealistic photograph, DSLR photo, 3D render, CGI, octane render, glossy 3D rendering, camera depth of field, lens bokeh, realistic skin texture, a posed group of people arranged for the camera, objects displayed on tables, trays, or plinths. Bold, confident, gallery-quality print design with a strong sense of place.
entries.restylePrompt: Restyle as a hand-pulled screenprinted gig poster with thick flat inks and halftone shading. Preserve the existing scene, subject placement, and every recognisable motif; do not add, remove, or rearrange subjects. Motif treatment: Turn each album motif into a bold screenprinted graphic element - chunky shapes, halftone tone, crisp knockouts - each one readable at a glance. Composition: One dominant central motif with the others orbiting it, tight and punchy like a classic gig poster. Palette: olive green, hot pink, deep violet-blue, black; three or four flat saturated ink colours on paper. Adults only; no gore, nudity, or sexual content. No text, letters, words, numbers, captions, logos, watermarks, or signage. Avoid: grid, contact sheet, tiled album covers, separate panels, photorealism, DSLR, 3D, CGI, bokeh, posed lineup, objects on plinths, giant central sculpture.

entries.shootDirection: a graffiti-covered back alley, the group mid-conversation walking toward a doorway camera - candid, not posed
entries.concept: Graffiti alley doorway walk-up, tight candid group portrait
entries.scene: In a narrow graffiti-covered back alley at blue hour, the four selected artists are caught mid-conversation walking toward a metal side doorway just a few steps from the camera, framed tightly enough that every face is large, crisp, and detailed while their bodies overlap naturally in motion. On the left, the young woman in the white shirt and black tie turns slightly inward as if listening, her dark hair bouncing softly; beside her, the auburn-haired woman in sculptural glasses and a dark outfit leans forward half a step, speaking with an intent but warm expression. Slightly deeper in the frame, the dark-haired man in the black high-neck top glances sideways toward them rather than at the lens, one shoulder brushing the tagged brick wall, while on the right the masked figure with bleached hair emerges through a haze of cool doorway light, the glowing X-eyed mask adding a surreal note against otherwise real street styling. The alley is damp and softly reflective, the pastel grade muting the spray-paint reds and blues into chalky tones, with diffused dusk light from above and a faint spill from the doorway creating calm contrast instead of harsh drama; the group feels intimate, unscripted, and genuinely in the same moment rather than posed in a lineup.
entries.prompt: Create one original, photorealistic 16:9 group photograph that brings together the musicians shown across the 4 reference images into a single shared real-world scene. Scene: In a narrow graffiti-covered back alley at blue hour, the four selected artists are caught mid-conversation walking toward a metal side doorway just a few steps from the camera, framed tightly enough that every face is large, crisp, and detailed while their bodies overlap naturally in motion. On the left, the young woman in the white shirt and black tie turns slightly inward as if listening, her dark hair bouncing softly; beside her, the auburn-haired woman in sculptural glasses and a dark outfit leans forward half a step, speaking with an intent but warm expression. Slightly deeper in the frame, the dark-haired man in the black high-neck top glances sideways toward them rather than at the lens, one shoulder brushing the tagged brick wall, while on the right the masked figure with bleached hair emerges through a haze of cool doorway light, the glowing X-eyed mask adding a surreal note against otherwise real street styling. The alley is damp and softly reflective, the pastel grade muting the spray-paint reds and blues into chalky tones, with diffused dusk light from above and a faint spill from the doorway creating calm contrast instead of harsh drama; the group feels intimate, unscripted, and genuinely in the same moment rather than posed in a lineup. Keep every person clearly recognisable and faithful to their reference photo - preserve each face, skin tone, hair, facial hair, glasses, and overall likeness exactly as shown. Do not invent anyone who is not in the reference photos. Keep this an intimate, well-composed group, not a crowd. Where a reference photo shows a band, feature only its single most recognisable member rather than every member. Frame the group tightly - close or medium-close - so every face is large in the frame, sharp, and richly detailed, never small or distant. Arrange them naturally within the scene as one connected group sharing the same moment and light, like a real posed or candid group photo. Vary posture, depth, and where people are looking - this is a caught moment, not a band-publicity lineup evenly spaced and facing the camera. Not separate cut-outs floating side by side, and never a grid, row, or panel of squares. Render photorealistically: real skin texture, true-to-life lighting and depth of field, captured as if shot on a professional camera. This is a real photograph, not an illustration, drawing, painting, cartoon, or render. Colour treatment: soft muted pastel grade, gentle and calm rather than saturated, drawing on powder blue, dusty pink, soft charcoal, chalk white, muted lavender. Mood: intimate, cool, quietly magnetic. Do not add any extra people beyond the reference subjects. Do not duplicate, merge, blend, or distort faces. Do not include any text, letters, words, numbers, captions, titles, logos, watermarks, or signage anywhere in the image. Avoid: readable text, letters, words, numbers, captions, titles, logos, watermarks, signage, grid layout, contact sheet, evenly tiled squares, raw album-cover thumbnails, collage of separate panels, illustration, illustrated, drawing, painting, cartoon, comic, anime, vector art, sketch, flat graphic style, extra people, background crowd, duplicated faces, cloned faces, merged faces, blended faces, morphed features, distorted faces, deformed hands, extra limbs. Photorealistic, sharp, high quality, flattering, with real depth and a strong sense of place.
